title: API rate limiter incorrectly throttling legitimate traffic — 429s on valid requests
body: API gateway is returning HTTP 429 Too Many Requests to customers who are within their contracted rate limits. Error rate on 429 responses is up 820% compared to baseline. Affected endpoints: POST /v2/orders, GET /v2/inventory, PUT /v2/products. Customers with tier-2 and tier-3 plans (10,000 and 50,000 req/min respectively) are being throttled as if they are on tier-1 (1,000 req/min). Tier-1 customers appear unaffected. The rate limiter reads plan tiers from a Redis cache with a 5-minute TTL. Cache hit rate has dropped from 98% to 12% — suspected cache invalidation issue or Redis connectivity problem causing the limiter to fall back to the lowest tier as a default. Three enterprise customers have opened P1 tickets directly with account managers.
